title: _Evangelion Symphony_ liner notes translation
wikitext:
[]: _Evangelion Symphony_ liner notes translation             Quote Post by gwern » Thu Oct 07, 2010 2:25 pm I did some quick Googling but nothing turned up here or on the wiki or on the general web. Back in '98, Yip reviewed the OST Evangelion Symphony ; the tracks themselves and a recording of the concert have been floating around for yonks, but Yip also paraphrased/translated some of the liner notes http://lists.onegeek.org/pipermail/oldeva/1998-January/008182.html : For example, it explains that THANTOS was orchestrated by Fukubu, of "Slayers the movie" and "Nadisco"'s fame, and reached another state of "musical evolution". It then went on to explain that THANTOS could mean (1) a phychology jargon meaning an urge for death (2) personified (anthropomorphized) death (3) god of death in Greek mythology. It then went on saying that to Anno it is the meaing (1) which made him choose that name as the title for the music. After that, the text explained in which circumstances the music was used in the TV (ep.19 and 20), and then compared it with the different versions of THANTOS in "Death" (titled "Tears for me") and "EOE" (titled "Intrusion of the substitute"). Another example is in its explanation of the "Symphony No.9" used in ep.24 "Last Massenger/Nagisa". It said "Director Hideaki Anno mentioned in one magazine that Kaworu can be considered as a symbol which overcame all the complexes held by Shinji. But it could also be viewed as the enlightenment of the introverted anime fans (Shinji) to bid goodbye to an image/object which was created by romanticizing and idealizing themselves (Kaworu), (here EVA-01 symbolized the anime "Evangelion") and breaks out from his own world and starts a new journey for a new self." With this significance, the text went on, it is then appropriate that this should be the first piece to be played in the concert.

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Mon Oct 11, 2010 4:25 am Wow, that's a very cool quote re: Kaworu. Nice find.

Article: _Evangelion Symphony_ liner notes translation
[]: _Evangelion Symphony_ liner notes translation             Quote Post by gwern » Thu Oct 07, 2010 2:25 pm I did some quick Googling but nothing turned up here or on the wiki or on the general web. Back in '98, Yip reviewed the OST Evangelion Symphony ; the tracks themselves and a recording of the concert have been floating around for yonks, but Yip also paraphrased/translated some of the liner notes http://lists.onegeek.org/pipermail/oldeva/1998-January/008182.html : For example, it explains that THANTOS was orchestrated by Fukubu, of "Slayers the movie" and "Nadisco"'s fame, and reached another state of "musical evolution". It then went on to explain that THANTOS could mean (1) a phychology jargon meaning an urge for death (2) personified (anthropomorphized) death (3) god of death in Greek mythology. It then went on saying that to Anno it is the meaing (1) which made him choose that name as the title for the music. After that, the text explained in which circumstances the music was used in the TV (ep.19 and 20), and then compared it with the different versions of THANTOS in "Death" (titled "Tears for me") and "EOE" (titled "Intrusion of the substitute"). Another example is in its explanation of the "Symphony No.9" used in ep.24 "Last Massenger/Nagisa". It said "Director Hideaki Anno mentioned in one magazine that Kaworu can be considered as a symbol which overcame all the complexes held by Shinji. But it could also be viewed as the enlightenment of the introverted anime fans (Shinji) to bid goodbye to an image/object which was created by romanticizing and idealizing themselves (Kaworu), (here EVA-01 symbolized the anime "Evangelion") and breaks out from his own world and starts a new journey for a new self." With this significance, the text went on, it is then appropriate that this should be the first piece to be played in the concert.
